name: context-driven-development
description: Use this skill when working with Conductor's context-driven
capabilities: Guide setup of context artifacts including product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md for new or existing projects
Explain the relationship between context artifacts and how to keep them synchronized
Provide checklists for validating context accuracy before implementation work
Support both greenfield projects with full control and brownfield projects with existing codebases
Document best practices for session continuity and multi-session development workflows
Identify and help avoid common anti-patterns like stale context and context sprawl
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and instructional content with no executable code, scripts, or network operations. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found no security patterns. The skill provides guidance on managing project context artifacts (product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md) for AI-assisted development workflows. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Teams struggle with inconsistent AI outputs and scattered project knowledge. This skill establishes structured context artifacts that ensure aligned AI behavior and preserve institutional memory across development sessions.
limitations: This skill provides methodology guidance only and does not execute code or manage files automatically
Requires team commitment to maintain context artifacts alongside code changes
Does not integrate directly with external tools or CI/CD systems without manual configuration
Best suited for projects using Conductor or similar context-driven development frameworks

useCases.title: Onboard new team members to established projects
useCases.description: Use context artifacts to rapidly bring new developers up to speed on product vision, tech stack decisions, and team workflows without requiring extensive pair programming or documentation hunting.

useCases.targetUser: Developers working with AI coding assistants
useCases.title: Establish consistent AI interaction patterns
useCases.description: Create a persistent context foundation that ensures AI assistants produce aligned outputs across different sessions and team members, reducing the need to re-explain project context repeatedly.

useCases.title: Transition from ad-hoc to structured development
useCases.description: Migrate existing projects from scattered documentation and implicit knowledge to a formalized context structure that survives team changes and informs future decisions.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Set up context for a new project
promptTemplates.prompt: I am starting a new project and want to implement context-driven development. Help me create the initial product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md files. Ask me questions about my product vision, technology preferences, and desired workflows to generate appropriate context artifacts.
promptTemplates.scenario: First-time user setting up a greenfield project

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Validate existing context accuracy
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my current context artifacts in the conductor/ directory. Check if product.md reflects implemented features, if tech-stack.md matches actual dependencies, and if workflow.md describes current practices. List any discrepancies and propose specific updates.
promptTemplates.scenario: Periodic context maintenance on an active project

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Migrate brownfield project to context-driven approach
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my existing codebase and extract implicit context to populate product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md. Identify current patterns, dependencies, and workflows. Then recommend any gaps between current practices and desired standards that should be documented.
promptTemplates.scenario: Establishing context management on an existing project

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Synchronize context after major feature completion
promptTemplates.prompt: A major feature has just been completed and merged. Guide me through updating all affected context artifacts: move features from planned to implemented in product.md, document any new dependencies in tech-stack.md, update tracks.md status, and ensure all cross-references remain accurate.

bestPractices: Read all context artifacts before starting work to ensure you have current information about product goals, technology constraints, and workflow requirements.
Update context artifacts as part of completing each track, not as a separate task. Make small incremental changes alongside code commits.
Include context artifact changes in code reviews to ensure the team validates documentation accuracy and maintains shared understanding.
antiPatterns: Stale context: Allowing documentation to become outdated leads to misaligned AI outputs and team confusion. Update context as part of every track completion.
Context sprawl: Creating new document types outside the defined artifact structure scatters information. Use the established structure and resist proliferation.
Over-specification: Documenting every detail makes context impossible to maintain. Focus only on decisions that affect AI behavior and team alignment.

faq.question: How often should I update context artifacts?
faq.answer: Update context artifacts whenever the information they contain changes. This typically happens when completing a feature track, adding dependencies, or modifying workflows. Treat context updates as part of the definition of done for each work item.

faq.question: What if my team already has documentation in a different format?
faq.answer: You can migrate existing documentation into the context artifact structure. Extract relevant information from existing docs and populate the appropriate artifacts. The key is having a single source of truth for each type of information, regardless of where it started.

faq.question: Is context-driven development suitable for solo developers?
faq.answer: Yes. While the methodology emphasizes team alignment, it also provides significant benefits for individual developers by preserving your own decisions and rationale over time, ensuring consistent AI interactions across sessions, and making it easier to resume work after interruptions.

faq.question: How do I handle context for multiple projects?
faq.answer: Each project should have its own independent context structure in a conductor/ directory at the project root. Context is project-specific because product vision, tech stack, and workflows differ between projects. Do not share context artifacts across projects.

faq.question: What happens if context artifacts conflict with each other?
faq.answer: Conflicts indicate outdated or inconsistent documentation. Resolve by determining which artifact has the correct current information, then update the conflicting artifacts to match. Use the product.md as the primary source for what you are building, and ensure tech-stack.md and workflow.md support those goals.

faq.question: Can I use this methodology without the Conductor tool?
faq.answer: Yes. The context-driven development methodology is tool-agnostic. While Conductor provides automation for setup and maintenance, you can manually create and maintain the context artifacts following the same structure and principles described in this skill.
